note: The 22 cases below (36716-36734, 36821-36823) were published directly to TestRail outside this repo's /fetch-section -> /migrate-section pipeline (created 2026-06-25 by a different account, and 2026-07-02 supplementing crown-icon nudge cases) and were never tracked locally until this backfill. grill_status/audit_status are honestly marked not-tracked-by-repo rather than assumed confirmed. The 2 stub cases (36827, 36828) are unrelated gap-cases discovered during Layout's grill that were rerouted to Layout and Plan Gates respectively per their own rewrite_notes -- kept here only because that's where they were originally filed. Case 36710 is a Cookie Banner Customization-page-shell case (sidebar tabs, banner preview, Publish Changes button) merged in from the now-deleted duplicate `cases-general.json` -- it structurally belongs to 04. Cookie Banner > Display & Layout, but there's no dedicated file for that section yet, so it's kept here rather than left orphaned.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Customization page renders with all required controls
cases.preconditions: User is on the Cookie Banner tab. The Customization page is open. The sidebar contains five tabs: General, Layout, Content, Colours, Custom CSS. The banner preview is visible on the right.

steps.content: Verify the General tab is selected in the Customization sidebar.
steps.expected: The General tab should be highlighted and the General section content should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the banner preview panel is visible to the right of the sidebar.
steps.expected: A live banner preview should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the Publish Changes button is visible at the bottom of the sidebar.
steps.expected: The "Publish Changes" button should be present but disabled by default until changes are made in the Customization settings.

cases.rewrite_notes: Schema-normalized from legacy raw-export format during audit; no source_case_ids existed prior — see source_case_ids/audit_note for what Phase 2/3 found.
cases.audit_note: Matches C275/C1392/C1398 (Webapp, AO/Admin/Editor) 1:1: sidebar tab list, "Publish Changes" button disabled by default, General shown by default. No load-bearing text distorted.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the General section renders with all required cards and controls
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector is set to GDPR (default).

steps.content: Verify the Consent Template card is visible at the top of the General section with the Law selector on its right.
steps.expected: The Consent Template card should be displayed with the Law selector dropdown visible.

steps.content: Verify the Geo-target banner card is visible below the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should be displayed with Worldwide, EU Countries & UK, and Select Countries options.

steps.content: Verify the Support IAB TCF v2.3 card is visible below the Geo-target banner card.
steps.expected: The Support IAB TCF v2.3 toggle should be displayed.

steps.content: Verify the Show advanced settings control is visible at the bottom of the General section.
steps.expected: The "Show advanced settings" control should be displayed as the last item in the section.

steps.content: Note the plan-gating state of the Law selector, Geo-target, and IAB TCF controls.
steps.expected:
- Free and Basic plans: the "GDPR & US State Laws" Law selector option, the "EU Countries & UK"/"Select Countries" Geo-target options, and the "Support IAB TCF v2.3" toggle are locked with an upgrade icon (Pro or higher required).
- Pro and Ultimate plans: all three are unlocked.
(See 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates for the locked-state verification.)

cases.rewrite_notes: Backfilled from live TestRail — this case was already published (created 2026-06-25 or 2026-07-02, before this repo's local tracking existed for this section). Content taken verbatim from the live case; source_case_ids matched against a fresh Suite 6 fetch of Cookie Banner > General done during this session's audit, based on title correspondence. Added a plan-gating step per the standing convention (migration-conventions.md §11 "Plan-gated render cases") on 2026-07-13, matching the fix already applied to Custom CSS, Colours, Cookie Manager, and Advanced Settings.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Consent Template defaults to the GDPR opt-in template
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab.

steps.content: Verify the Law selector in the Consent Template card shows "GDPR".
steps.expected: GDPR should be selected by default in the Law selector.

steps.content: Verify the banner preview displays the GDPR opt-in template.
steps.expected: The opt-in banner template should be shown in the preview panel.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting US State Laws applies the opt-out template
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector is set to GDPR (default).

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown in the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The dropdown should open showing GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws.

steps.content: Select "US State Laws".
steps.expected: The Law selector label should update to "US State Laws".

steps.content: Verify the banner preview switches to the opt-out template.
steps.expected: The opt-out banner template should appear in the preview panel.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the US State Laws opt-out banner should be active on the website.

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown in the Consent Template card.
steps.expected: The dropdown should open.

steps.content: Verify the dropdown shows exactly three options: GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws, each with a help icon.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that GDPR & US State Laws mode adds a Customize sub-dropdown switching between GDPR and US State Laws panels
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher).

steps.content: Click the Law selector dropdown and select "GDPR & US State Laws".
steps.expected: The Law selector label should update to "GDPR & US State Laws" and a "Customize" sub-dropdown should appear in the Consent Template card.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown.
steps.expected: GDPR and US State Laws should be listed as options.

steps.content: Verify GDPR is selected by default in the Customize dropdown.
steps.expected: The Customize dropdown should show "GDPR" and the banner preview should display the opt-in template.

steps.content: Select "US State Laws" from the Customize dropdown.
steps.expected: The Customize dropdown should update to "US State Laws" and the banner preview should switch to the opt-out template.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and both GDPR and US State Laws banners should be active on the website.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that selecting Select Countries restricts the banner to the chosen countries
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher). Law selector set to GDPR. (Select Countries behaves identically under US State Laws — same case covers both.)

steps.content: Under the Geo-target banner card, select "Select Countries".
steps.expected: A country selection dropdown should appear with country names and checkboxes.

steps.content: Select one or more countries from the dropdown.
steps.expected: The selected countries should show as checked.

steps.content: Click "Publish Changes".
steps.expected: Changes should be saved and the banner should display only in the selected countries on the website.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Consent expiration field rejects invalid values with a validation message
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Show advanced settings is expanded. The Consent expiration (days) field is visible.

steps.content: Attempt to type letters or special characters into the Consent expiration field.
steps.expected: The field should not accept non-numeric input.

steps.content: Enter an out-of-range number (e.g. 0 or 10000) into the field.
steps.expected: A validation message "Please enter a value between 1 and 9999" should appear below the field.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that clicking a geo-target crown icon opens the upgrade nudge
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. Law selector set to GDPR. EU Countries & UK and Select Countries show crown icons.

steps.content: Click the crown icon next to "EU Countries & UK" under the Geo-target banner card.
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge should appear showing "Display your banner only in select countries and regions" with an upgrade CTA button.

steps.content: Dismiss the nudge and click the crown icon next to "Select Countries".
steps.expected: The same upgrade nudge should appear.

steps.content: Set the Law selector to US State Laws.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should update to show Worldwide, United States, and Select Countries with crown icons on United States and Select Countries.

steps.content: Click the crown icon next to "United States".
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge should appear with an upgrade CTA button.

steps.content: Dismiss the nudge and click the crown icon next to "Select Countries".
steps.expected: The same upgrade nudge should appear.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that the Geo-target banner shows the correct options under GDPR & US State Laws
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > General tab. User is on a paid plan (Pro or higher). Law selector set to GDPR & US State Laws. The Customize sub-dropdown in the Consent Template card is visible, defaulting to GDPR.

steps.content: Verify the Geo-target banner card options while the Customize sub-dropdown is set to GDPR.
steps.expected: The card should display Worldwide, EU Countries & UK, and Select countries as options (no United States). Worldwide is selected by default.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown and select US State Laws.
steps.expected: The Geo-target banner card should update to display Worldwide, United States, and Select countries as options (no EU Countries & UK). Worldwide remains selected by default.

cases.rewrite_notes: Backfilled from live TestRail — created 2026-07-02. No corresponding Suite 6 case identified in this session's fetch; likely authored directly as a v2-only case (GDPR & US State Laws combined with Geo-target is a v2-specific interaction not present as a distinct Suite 6 scenario).

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that clicking the GDPR & US State Laws consent template option opens an upgrade nudge on free or basic plans
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Layout tab (or General tab where the Consent Template selector is visible). Account is on a free or basic plan. Consent Template is currently set to GDPR or US State Laws.

steps.content: Click the Consent Template dropdown to open the template switcher.
steps.expected: A dropdown appears with GDPR, US State Laws, and GDPR & US State Laws options listed.

steps.content: Click the GDPR & US State Laws option.
steps.expected: An upgrade nudge appears. The active consent template does not change — it remains at its previous value. No banner preview update occurs.

steps.content: Verify the upgrade nudge content and CTA.
steps.expected: The nudge indicates that GDPR & US State Laws requires a higher-tier plan. An upgrade CTA button is visible.

cases.rewrite_notes: Gap found during Layout grill (Pro account): On Pro plan the template switches directly — no nudge. Free/Basic nudge behavior is a Plan Gate. Published to 11. Billing & Upgrade > Plan Gates (section 1793) — not General.
cases.audit_note: Gap case, no source_case_ids — quotes dropdown option labels (GDPR, US State Laws, GDPR & US State Laws) and nudge/CTA wording. Already verified against the live app during grilling (grilled_at 2026-07-03); no separate live spot-check performed for this audit.

cases.title: [Cookie Banner] Verify that switching the law context in GDPR & US State Laws mode updates the available layout options
cases.preconditions: User is on Cookie Banner > Layout tab. Law selector is set to GDPR & US State Laws. The Customize sub-dropdown is visible in the Consent Template card, set to GDPR.

steps.content: Verify the Cookie Notice section while the Customize sub-dropdown is set to GDPR.
steps.expected: Box, Banner, and Popup layout options should be available. The Preference Centre section should be visible below the position options.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown and select US State Laws.
steps.expected: The Cookie Notice section should update to show only Box and Banner — Popup should not appear. The Preference Centre section should be replaced by an Opt-out center section.

steps.content: Click the Customize sub-dropdown and select GDPR.
steps.expected: The layout options should revert to GDPR state: Box, Banner, and Popup should be available and the Preference Centre section should be restored.

cases.rewrite_notes: Gap found during Layout grill (Pro account). Initially staged here but rerouted: layout option changes belong in Layout section. Published to Cookie Banner > Layout (section 1781) as C36827.
cases.audit_note: Gap case, no source_case_ids — quotes section/option labels (Box, Banner, Popup, Preference Centre, Opt-out center, GDPR, US State Laws). Already verified against the live app during grilling (grilled_at 2026-07-03); no separate live spot-check performed for this audit.

cases.title: Geo-target banner upgrade icon opens tooltip with 'Upgrade now' CTA on Basic plan
cases.preconditions: User is logged in on a Basic plan account. User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > General.

steps.content: Click the crown/upgrade icon next to 'EU Countries & UK' or 'Select Countries' under Geo-target banner.
steps.expected:
An upgrade tooltip appears showing:
- Headline: "Display your banner only in select countries and regions"
- Available in: "Pro and Ultimate plans"
- CTA button: "Upgrade now"

cases.rewrite_notes: Closes a Plan Gates gap found while auditing General: the existing published case (C36862) only covers the Free-plan 'Try Pro for free' CTA variant. Suite 6 has a distinct Basic-plan variant ('Upgrade now' CTA, same headline/availability text) that was never migrated. Mirrors C36862's shape/run_type exactly.

cases.title: IAB TCF v2.3 upgrade icon opens tooltip with 'Upgrade now' CTA on Basic plan
cases.preconditions: User is logged in on a Basic plan account. User is on Cookie Banner > Customisation sidebar > General.

steps.content: Click the 'Upgrade now' button next to 'Support IAB TCF v2.3'.
steps.expected:
An upgrade tooltip appears with:
- Headline: "Comply with IAB TCF and Google's requirements"
- Body: "Communicate user consent status to IAB vendors to enable Google ad personalization in EEA and the UK."
- Available in: "Pro and Ultimate plans"
- CTA button: "Upgrade now"

steps.content: Click 'Upgrade now'.
steps.expected: User is navigated to the Plans page showing all available subscription tiers (Free, Basic, Pro, Ultimate) with options to start a trial for each paid plan.

cases.rewrite_notes: Closes a Plan Gates gap found while auditing General: the existing published case (C36863) only covers the Free-plan 'Try Pro for free' CTA variant. Suite 6 has a distinct Basic-plan variant ('Upgrade now' CTA, same headline/body/availability text) that was never migrated. Mirrors C36863's shape/run_type exactly.
